title: 9 Herbal Creams For High Blood Pressure
intro_desc: Herbal creams for high blood pressure are gaining popularity as a natural way to manage the condition. These creams typically contain extracts from herbs like Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help relax blood vessels and improve circulation. Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, is another herb used in these creams, which has been shown to lower blood pressure by reducing the amount of stress hormones in the body. Ginkgo biloba, an herb known for its antioxidant properties, is also used to help improve blood flow and reduce the risk of blood clots.  Herbal teas, on the other hand, can help relieve high blood pressure by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. Drinking tea made from herbs like ginger, licorice root, and ginkgo biloba can help lower blood pressure by increasing the production of nitric oxide, a molecule that helps relax blood vessels and improve circulation. This can lead to a range of benefits, including improved cardiovascular health, increased energy levels, and a reduced risk of complications like heart disease and stroke. By incorporating herbal teas into your daily routine, you can take a proactive approach to managing your blood pressure and improving your overall health and wellbeing.
intro_image_alt: herbal creams for high blood pressure

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains a compound called gingerol, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. The active constituents in Zingiber officinale creams, including gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to help lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation. This is due to the vasodilatory properties of these compounds, which cause blood vessels to widen and allow blood to flow more easily. The anti-inflammatory properties of Zingiber officinale creams also help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, further contributing to its blood pressure-lowering effects. Regular use of Zingiber officinale creams has been found to help reduce systolic and diastolic blood pressure in individuals with high blood pressure.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for high blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat, stirring until smooth.
Add 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale extract and 1 tablespoon of aloe vera gel to the melted mixture.
Remove from heat and let cool until the mixture thickens. Stir in the vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into a container and let cool completely before using as a cream for high blood pressure.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains boivine, glycyrrhizin, and flavonoids as its active constituents. These compounds help to relax blood vessels, reduce inflammation, and improve blood flow to the heart. Glycyrrhizin, in particular, has been shown to increase the production of nitric oxide, a molecule that helps to widen blood vessels and lower blood pressure. The flavonoids present in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams also have antioxidant properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. By reducing inflammation and improving blood flow, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help to lower blood pressure in individuals with hypertension.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for high blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, 2 cups of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Grind the dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the ground Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and beeswax to the melted oil and butter mixture. Stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store the creams in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids and terpenoids. These compounds help to relax blood vessels, allowing for improved blood flow and lowering blood pressure. The flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba creams, specifically quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels. The terpenoids, such as bilobalide, also contribute to vasodilation, which is the widening of blood vessels and an increase in blood flow. As a result, Ginkgo biloba creams can help to reduce systolic and diastolic blood pressure by improving the function of blood vessels and reducing blood vessel stiffness.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for high blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract, and 10 drops of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Add the shea butter and stir until fully incorporated into the melted mixture.
Add the Ginkgo biloba extract and vitamin E oil, stirring well to combine. Pour the mixture into a clean container.
Let the mixture cool and solidify at room temperature. Once set, use a spatula to scoop out a small amount and apply to affected areas as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams contains valerenic acid, valeranone, and isovalerenic acid as its active constituents. These compounds have a relaxing effect on blood vessels, which helps to lower blood pressure. The sedative properties of valerian also help to reduce stress and anxiety, common causes of high blood pressure. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in valerian also have antioxidant properties, which can help to improve blood vessel function and reduce inflammation. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, valerian creams can help to alleviate high blood pressure symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for high blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of dried Valeriana officinalis root, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Add dried Valeriana officinalis root and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Add vitamin E oil and mix well. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng creams contains bioactive constituents like ginsenosides and eleutherosides, which have been shown to help lower blood pressure. Ginsenosides have a vasodilatory effect, which means they can widen blood vessels and improve blood flow, reducing the pressure inside the vessels. Eleutherosides have antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, making them more flexible and less prone to constricting. The combination of these two compounds in Panax ginseng creams can help improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure. Regular use of Panax ginseng creams may help reduce the risk of cardiovascular complications associated with high blood pressure.
remedies.image_alt: Panax ginseng herbal creams for high blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of Panax ginseng extract, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 drops of vitamin E oil.
Melt the beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the Panax ginseng extract to the melted mixture and stir well.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly. Then, add the distilled water and stir until well combined.

Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool completely. Once set, add the vitamin E oil and mix well. Store the Panax ginseng cream in the refrigerator and apply as needed to help lower high blood pressure.

remedies.remedy_desc: Coleus forskohlii creams contains forskolin, a bioactive constituent that helps lower high blood pressure. Forskolin works by increasing the production of nitric oxide in the blood vessels, causing them to relax and widen. This widening of blood vessels improves blood flow and reduces blood pressure. Additionally, forskolin also activates an enzyme called adenylate cyclase, which increases the production of cyclic AMP, a molecule that helps to relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure. As a result, Coleus forskohlii creams can be a useful natural remedy for people struggling with high blood pressure.
remedies.image_alt: Coleus forskohlii herbal creams for high blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of olive oil, and 1 teaspoon of Coleus forskohlii extract.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
In a separate bowl, mix shea butter and olive oil until well combined.
Add the Coleus forskohlii extract to the shea butter mixture and stir well.
Combine the melted coconut oil mixture with the shea butter mixture and stir until well combined. Pour into a container and let cool before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna creams contains flavonoids, such as quercetin and epicatechin, which have antioxidant properties. These flavonoids help to protect blood vessels from damage caused by free radicals, improving blood vessel function and reducing blood pressure. The cream also contains tannins, which have a soothing effect on blood vessels, causing them to dilate and improve blood flow. Additionally, Crataegus monogyna creams contain oligomeric proanthocyanidin (OPC), which helps to strengthen blood vessel walls, improving their ability to withstand blood pressure. By improving blood vessel function and structure, Crataegus monogyna creams help to lower high blood pressure.
remedies.image_alt: Crataegus monogyna herbal creams for high blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Crataegus monogyna leaves. Rinse them with water and pat dry.
Steep 1 cup of the leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes. Let it cool.
Strain the mixture and squeeze the leaves to get the liquid. Discard the leaves.
Mix 1 tablespoon of honey with the liquid to make it sweeter. Add 1/4 teaspoon of salt.
Store the liquid in an airtight container in the fridge. Take 2 tablespoons, 2-3 times a day, to help manage high blood pressure.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains the bioactive constituents curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. Curcumin has potent antioxidant properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, contributing to lower blood pressure. Demethoxycurcumin has anti-inflammatory properties that inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals, such as cytokines and prostaglandins, which can constrict blood vessels and increase blood pressure. Bisdemethoxycurcumin has been shown to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help lower blood pressure. By reducing inflammation and improving blood flow, Curcuma longa creams may help alleviate high blood pressure symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for high blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Get 1 cup of coconut oil and 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in the double boiler over low heat.
Add 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder (Curcuma longa) and 1 tablespoon of ginger powder to the melted mixture.
Stir well and continue heating until the mixture reaches 120°F to 140°F.
Remove from heat, let cool, and then pour into a container. Store in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa creams contains a rich source of bioactive compounds, including avenanthramides, avenacosides, and ferulic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, making it easier for the heart to pump blood. Avenanthramides, in particular, have been shown to inhibit the production of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), which is involved in the constriction of blood vessels. By inhibiting ACE, Avena sativa creams help to reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Regular use of Avena sativa creams may help to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of heart disease.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal creams for high blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Avena sativa flowers and 2 cups of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the coconut oil over low heat and add the dried Avena sativa flowers.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours, then strain it through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax with the strained oil and heat it over low heat until the beeswax is melted.
Pour the mixture into a container, let it cool, and store it in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent high blood pressure from forming. Some teas, like hawthorn and ginger, have natural ingredients that can lower blood pressure. They work by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation. Regularly drinking these teas may help keep blood pressure under control. This can be a healthy addition to a balanced lifestyle.
faq_safe: Some herbal teas, like hibiscus and rosemary, may help lower blood pressure. However, it's essential to be cautious with daily consumption. Drinking too much can lead to interactions with medications or worsen conditions like kidney problems. It's best to start with small amounts and monitor how your body reacts.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help lower high blood pressure, but it takes some time to see results. Studies show that some herbs like hawthorn and dandelion root can start working within 2-4 weeks. However, noticeable improvements in blood pressure may take 6-8 weeks to appear, and consistent use over several months can lead to sustained benefits.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for high blood pressure can be most helpful in the morning or early afternoon. This is because these times allow the tea's natural ingredients to work throughout your body during the day, potentially helping to lower blood pressure and improve circulation.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for high blood pressure. Some teas, like ginseng and licorice root, can raise blood pressure, making existing conditions worse. Others, like hawthorn and passionflower, can lower blood pressure, which may affect prescribed medication levels. It's essential to know the potential effects of herbal teas on your medication.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's a good idea to be cautious with herbal teas, especially if you have high blood pressure. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, are often considered safe, but others, like licorice root and ginseng, may not be suitable. Always read labels and research the herbs before drinking.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's a good idea to be cautious with herbal teas, especially if you have high blood pressure. Some herbal teas can lower blood pressure, which might be helpful for you, but others can cause a drop in blood pressure that could be a problem while breastfeeding.
